evals.prompt: I want to build a barcode scanning web app from scratch using SparkScan. I don't have any existing code yet.
evals.expected_output: The skill offers the pre-built sample as the starting point, asks whether the user is using React or vanilla JS, provides the relevant sample URL, and guides them through cloning it and replacing the license key and symbologies.

assertions.text: The pre-built sample is offered before any manual integration code is shown
The vanilla JS sample URL (ListBuildingSample) or the React sample URL (SparkScanReactSample) is mentioned
The user is asked whether they are using React or plain JS/TypeScript (or a similar framework question)
Replacing the license key placeholder is mentioned
Adjusting symbologies is mentioned

evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my web app. Here's my main file: empty-app.ts. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete SparkScan integration code into empty-app.ts, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions @scandit/web-datacapture-core and @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode npm packages)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with libraryLocation and moduleLoaders
barcodeCaptureLoader() is present in moduleLoaders
License key placeholder (e.g. ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY or similar) is present
SparkScanSettings is created with new SparkScanSettings()
enableSymbologies is called on settings
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
SparkScan.forSettings( is present
addListener with didScan callback is present
session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is accessed inside didScan
SparkScanView.forElement( is present
document.body.style or equivalent sets position: fixed or position: absolute on the mount element before SparkScanView.forElement is called
The mount element has non-zero width and height set (e.g. 100% or explicit px values)
The SparkScan listener is stored in a named variable so it can be removed
removeListener is called on the SparkScan instance in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
prepareScanning() is called
stopScanning() is available for cleanup

evals.prompt: I have a basic React app and need to integrate SparkScan barcode scanning. I want to scan EAN-13 and Code 128. Here's my component: empty-react-app.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates SparkScan into the React component using the web component pattern with <spark-scan-view>, proper React hooks, and cleanup on unmount.

assertions.text: SparkScanView.register() is called before the component
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with moduleLoaders and barcodeCaptureLoader()
License key placeholder is present
SparkScanSettings is created and enableSymbologies is called
Symbology.EAN13UPCA and Symbology.Code128 are enabled
SparkScan.forSettings( is present
addListener with didScan callback is present
<spark-scan-view is used as JSX web component
useEffect is used for initialization
stopScanning() is called in the useEffect cleanup return
removeListener is called on the SparkScan instance in the useEffect cleanup return
Setup checklist mentions @scandit/web-datacapture-core and @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode npm packages
A container element (div or equivalent) wrapping <spark-scan-view> has position: fixed or position: absolute set
The container element wrapping <spark-scan-view> has non-zero width and height (e.g. 100% or explicit px values)

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my app, I want to reject it if the data is '123456789' and show an error message 'Wrong barcode' for 30 seconds before resuming scanning. All other barcodes should show success feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill modifies the existing feedbackDelegate to reject barcodes with data '123456789', returning SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback with 30-second resume delay and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback for valid ones.

assertions.text: SparkScanFeedbackDelegate is present
getFeedbackForBarcode method is implemented
sparkScanView.feedbackDelegate is assigned
SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( is present
30_000 or 30000 is set as the resume delay on the error feedback
SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() is returned for non-rejected barcodes
The rejection condition checks barcode.data === "123456789" or equivalent
Existing addListener with didScan callback is preserved
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: My app is used in a warehouse where workers wear gloves. Enable hardware trigger button support so they can scan using physical device buttons.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables hardwareTriggerEnabled on SparkScanViewSettings and passes the settings to SparkScanView.forElement.

assertions.text: isHardwareTriggerEnabled = true is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanViewSettings instance is created and configured before being passed to SparkScanView.forElement
SparkScanView.forElement receives the configured SparkScanViewSettings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to hide the standard SparkScan trigger button and instead use a custom button in my app's UI. When the custom button is clicked, scanning should start. When clicked again while scanning, it should pause.
evals.expected_output: The skill hides the standard trigger button, creates a custom HTML button element, and uses SparkScanViewUiListener to track the actual scanner state. The click handler calls startScanning() or pauseScanning() based on current state — not a local toggle — so it stays correct after the engine auto-idles following a scan.

assertions.text: sparkScanView.isTriggerButtonVisible = false is set
A custom button element is created (document.createElement("button") or equivalent)
addEventListener("click" is used to handle button clicks
SparkScanViewUiListener is implemented with didChangeViewState
uiListener is assigned on sparkScanView
Current view state is tracked (e.g. a variable updated inside didChangeViewState)
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called when state is not active
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called when state is active
The button is appended to the DOM (appendChild or similar)
The click handler is stored in a named variable or function so it can be removed
removeEventListener is called on the button in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
sparkScanView.uiListener is set to null in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need a custom trigger button that changes text and behavior based on the scanner state. Show 'START SCANNING' in IDLE state and 'STOP SCANNING' in ACTIVE state.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements SparkScanViewUiListener, tracks view state changes, and updates the button text and behavior based on state. Both the click handler and uiListener are stored as named references and removed in cleanup.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewUiListener is present
uiListener is assigned on sparkScanView (via setListener or property)
didChangeViewState callback is implemented
State comparison for idle or initial state is present
State comparison for active state is present
Button text is set to 'START SCANNING' for idle/inactive states
Button text is set to 'STOP SCANNING' for active state
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called for idle/inactive states
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called for active state
The click handler is stored in a named variable or function so it can be removed
removeEventListener is called on the button in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
sparkScanView.uiListener is set to null in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the trigger button is hidden, I want to let users click on the page background to pause scanning. The click handler should only be active when scanning is active.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a click event listener on a background element, implements a handler that calls sparkScanView.pauseScanning(), and uses SparkScanViewUiListener to enable/disable the handler based on scanner state. Both the click handler and uiListener are stored as named references and removed in cleanup.

assertions.text: addEventListener("click" is used on a background or container element
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called in the click handler
SparkScanViewUiListener is present
didChangeViewState callback is implemented
Click handler is enabled or added when viewState is active
Click handler is disabled or removed when viewState is not active
The click handler is stored in a named variable or function so it can be removed
removeEventListener is called on the background element in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
sparkScanView.uiListener is set to null in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to set up SparkScan with an 'Aim-to-Scan' workflow for precise barcode scanning in crowded environments. The preview should always be visible (even when not scanning), target mode should always be enabled, and single scan behavior should be used.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with persistent preview behavior and target mode scanning, then passes these settings to SparkScanView.forElement.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created
defaultScanningMode is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanScanningModeTarget is used for the scanning mode
SparkScanScanningBehavior.single is used
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.persistent is used
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView.forElement
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm building a high-volume warehouse scanning app where we need rapid, continuous barcode scanning without pausing between items. Set up SparkScan with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and PERSISTENT preview so the camera stays visible the whole time.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with continuous scanning behavior and persistent preview behavior, keeping the scanner active and camera always visible.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created
defaultScanningMode is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanScanningBehavior.continuous is used (not single)
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.persistent is used (not default)
SparkScanScanningMode wrapper is used (e.g., SparkScanScanningModeDefault or similar)
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView.forElement
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to minimize the UI footprint of SparkScan in my app. Configure it so the camera preview only appears when actively scanning, not when idle. All other times it should be hidden.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with default preview behavior (SparkScanPreviewBehavior.default), making the preview appear on-demand only during active scanning.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created
defaultScanningMode is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.default is used (not persistent)
SparkScanScanningMode wrapper is used with the preview behavior
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView.forElement
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need a high-volume warehouse app with minimal UI footprint. Set up SparkScan with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and POPUP preview behavior, so scanning happens rapidly and the preview only shows when needed.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with continuous scanning behavior combined with default preview behavior (SparkScanPreviewBehavior.default).

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created
defaultScanningMode is set on SparkScanViewSettings
SparkScanScanningBehavior.continuous is used
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.default is used (not persistent)
SparkScanScanningMode wrapper combines both continuous behavior and default preview
The configured SparkScanViewSettings is passed to SparkScanView.forElement
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, feedbackDelegate, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to add DataMatrix and QR symbology support to my scanning app. Also change the barcode rejection to reject any barcode with data shorter than 5 characters.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds DataMatrix and QR to enableSymbologies, modifies isBarcodeRejected to check data length, and preserves all existing class structure and rendering logic.

assertions.text: Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
Symbology.QR is enabled
Existing Symbology.EAN13UPCA is preserved
Existing Symbology.Code128 is preserved
isBarcodeRejected logic checks barcode.data length < 5 or equivalent
SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback is still used for rejected barcodes
AppController class structure is preserved
connect() and disconnect() lifecycle methods are preserved
renderList() method is preserved
scannedItems Map is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to show the image of the scanned barcode next to each item in my list. How do I capture the frame image when a barcode is scanned?
evals.expected_output: The skill updates didScan to use frameData.toBlob('image/jpeg', 0.3) without awaiting it, uses .then()/.catch() to add the result with or without the image, updates ScannedItem to include an image field, and preserves all existing class structure.

assertions.text: frameData.toBlob("image/jpeg", 0.3) is called (or equivalent JPEG blob conversion)
toBlob is NOT awaited — .then()/.catch() is used instead
A success path adds the scan result with the blob
A .catch() fallback adds the scan result with null or without an image
Rejected barcodes are skipped before attempting blob conversion
ScannedItem interface (or equivalent) includes an image field of type Blob or Blob | null
SymbologyDescription is used or imported to get a readable symbology name
Existing AppController class structure, connect(), disconnect(), and renderList() are preserved

evals.prompt: I need to add custom feedback to my React SparkScan app. Reject barcodes with data '999' and show an error message 'Item not found' with a 10 second resume delay. Show success for all other barcodes.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a feedbackDelegate with SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback for rejected barcodes and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback for valid ones, integrated into the React component via the <spark-scan-view> web component.

assertions.text: SparkScanFeedbackDelegate or feedbackDelegate object is present
getFeedbackForBarcode is implemented
SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback is returned for barcode.data === '999'
Error message 'Item not found' is set on SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback
10_000 or 10000 is set as the resume delay
SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback is returned for valid barcodes
feedbackDelegate is passed to <spark-scan-view> component or set via ref
useMemo is used for the feedbackDelegate to avoid unnecessary re-renders
Existing React hooks (useEffect, useRef, useState, useCallback) are preserved
Existing sparkScanListener and initialization logic are preserved
